plus.analyst_notes:
Closes https://github.com/vz-risk/VCDB/issues/23570
Encoded by AI — encode-veris-incident skill version 20260713T202114Z

Sources consulted: McShane & Brady law firm (https://mcshanebradylaw.com/southern-california-univ-health-sciences-data-breach/); ClaimDepot summary (https://www.claimdepot.com/data-breach/southern-california-university-of-health-sciences-2026); Federman & Sherwood investigation notice (https://www.federmanlaw.com/blog/southern-california-university-of-health-sciences-data-breach-investigated-by-federman-sherwood/).

Timeline: An unauthorized actor accessed and copied files from the university's network between March 23-24, 2026. The university became aware of the unauthorized activity on March 24, 2026 (same day as the end of the access window). Affected individuals notified on or about May 18, 2026. Breach disclosed to AGs in California and Maine on May 19, 2026. Using March 23, 2026 as the incident begin date.

NAICS: Pipeline suggested 6221 (General Medical and Surgical Hospitals) but SCU is primarily an educational institution (chiropractic and health sciences university in Whittier, CA). Coded as 6113 (Colleges, Universities, and Professional Schools) which is more accurate. PHIDBR2019 label ignored per VCDB policy.

Data: Names and Social Security numbers of 2,206 individuals were confirmed compromised. ClaimDepot gives precise count of 2,206. Personal for names; Sensitive Personal for SSNs.

Initial access vector and hacking variety not specified by sources. Motive inferred as Financial (mass data theft typical of financially-motivated threat actors).

Employee count: SCU is a small private health sciences university (~200-500 employees); coded 101 to 1000.

Discovery method: The university detected unauthorized activity on March 24 but the specific detection mechanism is not disclosed; coded Internal - Unknown.

DBIR year 2027 derived from March 2026 incident begin date (Jan-Oct → Y+1 = 2027).

event_chain.summary: Unauthorized actor gains initial access to university network by unknown method
Actor accesses and copies files containing names and Social Security numbers of 2,206 individuals

summary: Between March 23-24, 2026, an unauthorized actor accessed and copied files stored on the Southern California University of Health Sciences network. The breach affected 2,206 individuals and exposed names and Social Security numbers. The university detected the unauthorized activity on March 24 and launched an investigation with outside cybersecurity specialists. Affected individuals were notified in May 2026.

victim.victim_id: Southern California University of Health Sciences
